faq.desc: Answers to the most frequently asked questions by our clients.
faq.q1: How long does it take to build a website?
faq.a1: For simple business card websites, we prepare the first design version (mockup) within 72 hours. After the client approves the design, we deliver the finished website also within 72 hours. More complex projects (online stores, elaborate portals, custom functionalities) are completed in 3-6 weeks, depending on the scope of work.
faq.q2: Can I edit the website content myself?
faq.a2: Yes! In Business and Enterprise packages, we install a CMS system (e.g., WordPress) that allows easy content editing without programming knowledge. We also provide panel training.
faq.q3: What is needed to start a project?
faq.a3: We need from you: company logo, content (or commission us to write it), photos (or we'll use stock), and a brief with information about the company and website goals. We'll guide you through the entire process.
faq.q4: Do you offer hosting and a domain?
faq.a4: Yes, we can help with purchasing a domain and hosting, as well as their configuration. We work with trusted hosting service providers in Poland.
faq.a5: We typically work on a model of: 50% advance before starting work, 50% after project approval. For larger projects, we can arrange milestone-based payments. We issue VAT invoices.

article1.title: HTML vs WordPress Website - What to Choose? | Dekada72H Blog

meta.desc: HTML or WordPress website? Learn the pros and cons of both solutions. Find out which is better for your business in terms of speed, security, and SEO.

article1.heading: HTML vs WordPress Website - What to Choose for Your Business?
article1.subheading: A detailed comparison of both solutions to help you make the best decision.

card.title: HTML vs WordPress - What to Choose for Your Business?
card.excerpt: A detailed comparison of HTML and WordPress websites in terms of speed, security, SEO, and costs. Find out which solution is better for your business.

article1.intro1: Are you facing the decision of creating a website for your business and wondering which technology to choose? This is one of the most important questions to ask yourself before investing money in your online presence. The two most popular approaches are a website written in pure HTML/CSS or a website built on WordPress. Each has its pros and cons - and in this article, we'll honestly discuss them.
article1.intro2: As an agency with over 10 years of experience, we have created many websites using both technologies. We see every day how the choice of platform affects our clients' business success. That's why we want to share our knowledge with you so you can make an informed decision.

h2.security: Security - Where is Your Data Safer?
h2.seo: SEO - Which Website Ranks Better on Google?
h2.design: Unique Design - Who Offers More Possibilities?
h2.whentochoose: When to Choose HTML vs WordPress?
h2.conclusion: Our Opinion - Why We Choose HTML

whathtml.p1: An HTML website (often called a static website) is a site written by hand in HTML, CSS, and possibly JavaScript code. Every element of the page is designed and coded from scratch by a developer. There is no content management system, no plugins, no database - just clean, optimized code.
whathtml.p2: Think of it this way: an HTML website is like a tailor-made suit. Every element is fitted exactly to your needs. Nothing is excessive, nothing is unnecessary.

whatwp.p1: WordPress is a content management system (CMS) that powers over 40% of all websites in the world. It runs on a server using PHP and a MySQL database. Users can manage content through an admin panel, install themes (design templates) and plugins (additional functionality) without any programming knowledge.
whatwp.p2: Continuing the analogy - WordPress is like a suit bought in a store. You can choose the size and color, but it's not tailored specifically for you. It's universal and convenient, but it may not fit perfectly for every situation.

caption.code: Clean HTML code - clear, fast, and fully under the developer's control.
caption.security: Website security is a key aspect that affects customer trust.
caption.seo: Good SEO optimization is the key to visibility in search engines.
caption.design: Unique design sets your company apart from the competition.

speed.p1: There's no debate here - HTML websites are significantly faster. Why? Because when someone visits your site, the server simply sends the ready HTML file to the browser. There's no processing, no database queries, no generating pages on the fly.
speed.p2: WordPress, on the other hand, has to run a PHP script every time, connect to the database, fetch content, process it through the theme and all active plugins, and only then send the finished page to the user. All of this takes time.
speed.highlight: Google research shows that 53% of mobile users leave a site if it takes longer than 3 seconds to load. Every second of delay reduces conversion by about 7%. Speed is not a luxury - it's a necessity.
speed.p3: Of course, WordPress can be sped up with caching plugins, CDN, and optimized hosting. But why pay more for solutions to a problem that simply doesn't exist with an HTML website?

security.p1: This is an area where HTML websites have a huge advantage. A static website has no database, no login panel, no plugins with potential vulnerabilities. The attack surface is minimal - because there's practically nothing to attack.
security.p2: WordPress is a completely different story. As the most popular CMS in the world, it's also the most common target for hackers. The statistics are concerning:
security.li1: It's estimated that about 13,000 WordPress sites are hacked every day - that's 9 attacks per minute
security.li2: WordPress accounts for 90% of all hacked CMS-based websites
security.li3: 99.4% of security vulnerabilities in the WordPress ecosystem come from themes and plugins
security.li4: 52% of all vulnerabilities are caused by outdated plugins
security.p3: This doesn't mean WordPress is inherently insecure - the core system itself is well protected. The problem lies in add-ons: themes, plugins, and neglected updates. If you choose WordPress, you need to regularly update the system, monitor security, and invest in professional technical support.

seo.p1: WordPress has a reputation for being SEO-friendly, mainly thanks to plugins like Yoast SEO or Rank Math, which make content optimization easier. And indeed - for people without technical knowledge, this is a huge help.
seo.p2: But the truth is that an HTML website can achieve exactly the same (and often better) SEO results. Why? Because:
seo.li1: Loading speed is one of Google's key ranking factors - and HTML pages load lightning fast
seo.li2: Clean code without unnecessary scripts and styles is easier for search engine bots to index
seo.li3: Full control over meta tags, heading structure, schema markup, and all technical SEO elements
seo.li4: No unnecessary code - WordPress generates a lot of excess code from themes and plugins, which slows down indexing
seo.p3: At our agency, we take care of comprehensive SEO for every project - regardless of technology. But we have to admit that HTML websites give us much more freedom in technical optimization.

costs.p1: At first glance, WordPress may seem cheaper - after all, the system itself is free, and the internet is full of cheap templates. But that's only part of the picture. The real costs reveal themselves over time:
costs.p2: Over 3-5 years, an HTML website is usually cheaper to maintain. There's no risk of a plugin update breaking your site on Friday evening, you don't have to pay for premium security plugins, and you don't need expensive hosting with PHP support. Check our pricing to see what packages we offer.

design.p1: If you want your website to look unique and not resemble thousands of other sites on the internet, an HTML website gives you unlimited possibilities. Every pixel, every animation, every element is designed specifically for you.
design.p2: WordPress relies on themes (templates) that are universal by definition. Even if you customize colors and content, the basic structure and layout remain the same as thousands of other businesses using the same theme. Page builders (Elementor, Divi) offer more flexibility, but they also generate huge amounts of unnecessary code, which slows down the site.
design.p3: Check out our portfolio - every project is different because each was created from scratch, tailored to the client's brand character. This is a value that cannot be achieved with any ready-made template.

choose.html1: You run a business and need a professional online presence
choose.html2: You care about speed, security, and unique appearance
choose.html3: You don't plan to add new content daily
choose.html4: You want a website that "just works" without constant maintenance
choose.html5: You want to stand out from the competition
choose.wp1: You run a blog or news site with frequent publications
choose.wp3: You want to manage content yourself without a developer's help
choose.wp4: You have a limited initial budget and accept higher maintenance costs

conclusion.p1: As an agency specializing in creating websites from scratch, we have a clear position: for most businesses, an HTML website is the better choice. Not because WordPress is bad - it's a solid platform with many uses. But for a typical business that needs a professional online presence, the benefits of an HTML website are undeniable:
conclusion.highlight: Faster website = better Google rankings = more customers. More secure website = peace of mind. Unique website = standing out from competition. It's simple math.
conclusion.p2: Every project of ours is created from scratch, with a specific business and its goals in mind. We don't use templates, we don't install unnecessary plugins, we don't compromise. The result? Websites that load in a fraction of a second, look exceptional, and attract customers. See for yourself in our portfolio.

article2.title: Landing Page - What Is It and Why Do You Need One? | Dekada72H Blog

meta.desc: What is a landing page and why does your business need one? Learn how an effective landing page can increase sales and acquire new customers.

article2.heading: Landing Page - What Is It and Why Does Your Business Need One?
article2.subheading: A complete guide to landing pages that turn visitors into customers.

card.title: Landing Page - What Is It and Why Does Your Business Need One?
card.excerpt: A complete guide to landing pages. Learn how an effective landing page can increase sales and acquire new customers from ads.

article2.intro1: Spending money on Google or Facebook ads but not seeing results? Your website looks good but doesn't generate inquiries from customers? The problem might not be the ads themselves, but where you're sending the traffic. Most businesses make the same mistake - directing potential customers to the homepage instead of a dedicated landing page.
article2.intro2: In this article, we'll explain exactly what a landing page is, why it's so effective, and how it can help your business acquire more customers. As an agency with over 10 years of experience in creating websites from scratch, we see the differences every day between businesses that use landing pages and those that don't.

h2.why: Why Does a Landing Page Work Better Than a Regular Website?
h2.elements: What Must an Effective Landing Page Include?
h2.vs: Landing Page vs Business Website - When to Choose Which?
h2.html: Why Do We Create Landing Pages in Pure HTML?
h2.process: How Do We Create Landing Pages at Dekada72H?

what.p1: A landing page is a standalone web page created for one specific purpose - to convert a visitor into a customer. Unlike a regular business website that presents the entire offer and company history, a landing page focuses on one service, one product, or one action you want the user to take.
what.p2: Think of it this way: your homepage is like a shopping mall - many stores, many entrances, many distractions. A landing page is like a shop with one product in the window and a clear path to checkout. You know exactly why the customer came, and you guide them straight to the goal.

caption.analytics: An effective landing page is one that can be measured and optimized for conversions.
caption.design: Every element of a landing page is designed to guide the user toward conversion.
caption.code: Clean, optimized code is the foundation of a fast landing page.

why.p1: The statistics speak for themselves. Businesses that use dedicated landing pages achieve significantly better results than those that direct traffic to their homepage.
why.p2: Why is that? Because a landing page eliminates distractions. On a regular website, users can click the menu, browse the blog, check the portfolio, read the terms - and eventually forget why they came in the first place. A landing page gives them one option: fill out the form, call, or buy. And that's exactly why it converts better.
why.highlight: The golden rule of landing pages: one page = one goal = one call to action. The fewer options you give the user, the more conversions you'll get.

elements.intro: Every effective landing page consists of several key elements. Here's what must be on your landing page to deliver results:
elements.li1: Compelling headline - You have 3 seconds to convince the visitor to stay. The headline must clearly communicate the benefit, e.g., "Increase sales by 200% with a professional website".
elements.li2: Benefits, not features - The customer doesn't buy "a responsive website with SEO". They buy "more customers from the internet". Write about what the customer will gain, not what you do.
elements.li3: Social proof - Customer reviews, company logos, case studies, numbers. People trust other people more than advertisements.
elements.li4: Clear call to action (CTA) - The "Request a free quote" button must be visible, high-contrast, and repeated several times on the page.
elements.li5: Contact form - The shorter, the better. Name, email, phone - that's enough. Every additional field reduces conversion by 10-25%.
elements.li6: No navigation menu - This is the key difference between a landing page and a regular website. We remove the menu so the user has nowhere to escape.

vs.p1: A landing page and a business website are two different tools for two different purposes. They don't replace each other - they complement each other.
vs.p2: A business website builds your image, presents the full offer, and acts as an online business card. A customer can visit it to learn about your company, check the portfolio, read about the team.
vs.p3: A landing page serves one purpose: conversion. You use it when launching an ad campaign, promoting a specific service, or wanting to collect contacts of potential customers.
vs.li1: You're running an ad campaign (Google Ads, Facebook Ads)
vs.li2: You want to promote one specific service or product
vs.li3: You're collecting contacts of potential customers (lead generation)
vs.li4: You're offering a free quote, consultation, or e-book
vs.li5: You're organizing an event or webinar

mistakes.intro: We've seen hundreds of landing pages - from our clients and their competitors. Here are the mistakes that most often kill conversion:
mistakes.li1: Too much text - a landing page is not an article. Short, specific sentences work better than walls of text
mistakes.li2: No clear CTA - if the user doesn't know what to do, they won't do anything. The button must be visible and unambiguous
mistakes.li3: Slow loading - every second of delay loses 7% of conversions. That's why we create landing pages in pure HTML, without unnecessary code
mistakes.li4: Inconsistency with the ad - if the ad promises "free quote in 24h", the landing page must repeat that. Consistency builds trust
mistakes.li5: No mobile version - over 60% of traffic comes from phones. A landing page must look perfect on every device
mistakes.li6: Form too long - every additional field is a barrier. Only require what you truly need

html.p1: As we wrote in our HTML vs WordPress article, websites built from scratch have enormous advantages. For landing pages, these advantages are even more important because every millisecond of loading and every pixel of design directly affects conversion.
html.p2: A landing page in pure HTML loads in under 1 second - that's 3-5x faster than a typical landing page built in WordPress with Elementor. With advertising budgets of thousands of zlotys per month, this speed difference translates into real money.
html.highlight: Imagine: you spend 5000 PLN per month on ads. A faster landing page increases conversion by 20%. That's additional customer inquiries for the same money. The investment in a good landing page pays for itself in the first month.
html.p3: Additionally, a landing page built from scratch gives us full control over every element. We can test different headline variants, button colors, element layouts - and measure which variant delivers better results. This is something that's not easily achieved with ready-made templates. Check our portfolio to see examples of our work.

process.intro: Our landing page creation process is simple but effective:
process.li1: Goal analysis - We discuss your business, target audience, and campaign goal. Understanding "for whom" and "why" is the foundation of an effective landing page.
process.li2: Graphic design - We create a unique design tailored to your brand. Every element is designed with conversion in mind.
process.li3: Coding from scratch - We write clean HTML/CSS/JS - no templates, no WordPress, no unnecessary code. Result: lightning-fast loading.
process.li4: Launch and monitoring - We launch the page, configure analytics and conversion tracking. We monitor results and optimize.

conclusion.p1: A landing page is one of the most effective marketing tools your business can have. If you run advertising campaigns or want to acquire customers online, a dedicated landing page can be the difference between a campaign that burns through the budget and one that delivers real inquiries and sales.
conclusion.p2: The key is a strategic approach - a "pretty page" is not enough. You need a page designed for conversion, written in clean code for maximum speed, and optimized for a specific business goal. That's exactly what we do at Dekada72H.
conclusion.highlight: A good landing page is not a cost - it's an investment. An investment that pays for itself with every new customer who fills out the form or picks up the phone.

cta.title: Need a Landing Page That Sells?
cta.desc: Talk to us about your project. We'll create a landing page that turns visitors into customers. The quote is always free.

article3.title: SEO & Website Positioning - How to Outrank Your Competition in Google? | Dekada72H Blog

meta.desc: What is SEO and how can website positioning help your business? A complete guide to search engine optimization - from basics to advanced strategies.

article3.heading: SEO & Website Positioning - How to Outrank Your Competition in Google?
article3.subheading: A complete SEO guide - from basics to advanced strategies that will take your website to the top of search results.

card.title: SEO & Website Positioning - How to Outrank Your Competition in Google?
card.excerpt: A complete SEO guide. Learn how website positioning can increase your business visibility in Google and attract more customers.

article3.intro1: You have a website but customers can't find you on Google? Your competition ranks high in search results while you wonder why your business is invisible? The answer is simple: SEO, or search engine optimization. It's the process that determines whether customers will find your business when they search for the services or products you offer.
article3.intro2: In this article, we'll explain what SEO is, how it works, and why it's absolutely crucial for every business that wants to acquire customers online. As an agency with over 10 years of experience in building websites from scratch, we combine the ability to create fast, clean websites with SEO expertise - because one without the other doesn't work.

h2.what: What Is SEO and Why Is It So Important?
h2.technical: Technical SEO - The Foundation Many Forget
h2.local: Local SEO - The Key for Service Businesses
h2.mistakes: Most Common SEO Mistakes That Kill Your Visibility
h2.why: Why Is It Worth Investing in Professional SEO?
h2.process: How Do We Do SEO at Dekada72H?

what.p1: SEO (Search Engine Optimization) is a set of actions aimed at improving your website's visibility in organic (free) Google search results. When someone types "marketing agency" or "website development" into Google, SEO determines which websites appear on the first page of results.
what.p2: Why is this so important? Because 93% of online experiences start with a search engine. If your business isn't on the first page of Google, you practically don't exist for most potential customers. Nobody browses the second page of results - it's like having a shop on a street where nobody walks.
what.highlight: SEO is not a one-time action but an ongoing process. But unlike ads, SEO results persist long after the work is done. It's like building real estate on the internet - once a position is built, it works for you for years.

stat.firstpage: Users don't scroll past the 1st page
stat.close: Lead conversion from SEO vs 1.7% from ads
stat.visit: Local searchers visit a business within 24h
stat.purchase: Local searches result in a purchase

onpage.intro: On-page SEO is everything you can do directly on your website to improve its position in Google. It's the foundation of every SEO strategy and the first thing we start with when working with a client.
onpage.li1: Page Titles (Title Tags) - Every page must have a unique, descriptive title containing keywords. It's the first element that Google and users see in search results.
onpage.li2: Meta Descriptions - A short page description visible in Google results. A well-written meta description can increase click-through rate (CTR) by up to 30%.
onpage.li3: Headings (H1, H2, H3) - The heading structure helps Google understand the content hierarchy on the page. Every page should have exactly one H1 and logically nested H2 and H3 tags.
onpage.li4: High-Quality Content - Google rewards websites with valuable, unique content that answers user questions. Length, quality, and freshness of content matter enormously.
onpage.li5: Internal Linking - Connecting pages to each other helps Google understand the site structure and distribute "SEO power" across pages.

caption.analytics: SEO data analysis allows you to measure positioning effectiveness and optimize your strategy.
caption.strategy: Effective SEO is a combination of on-page, technical, and off-page strategies.

technical.intro: Even the best content won't help if your website is slow, unresponsive, or poorly built technically. Technical SEO is a set of actions that ensure Google can properly read and index your website.
technical.li1: Page loading speed - Google has officially confirmed that loading time is a ranking factor. Every second of delay means losing positions and customers
technical.li2: Responsiveness (Mobile-First) - Since 2019, Google primarily evaluates websites based on their mobile version. A website that looks bad on a phone loses rankings
technical.li3: SSL Certificate (HTTPS) - A secure connection is a Google requirement. Websites without SSL are marked as "not secure" and lose rankings
technical.li4: Structured Data (Schema Markup) - Special code that helps Google understand page content and display rich search results
technical.li5: Sitemap (XML Sitemap) - A file that informs Google about all pages of your website and facilitates indexing
technical.li6: Image optimization - Compression, proper formats (WebP), and alt attributes - all of this affects speed and visibility in Google Images
technical.highlight: That's why we create websites in pure HTML instead of WordPress. A website built from scratch loads in under 1 second, has perfect code, and meets all Google technical requirements without needing plugins.

offpage.p1: Off-page SEO refers to actions outside your website that build its authority in Google's eyes. The most important element is backlinks - references to your website from other sites.
offpage.p2: Think of it like recommendations. If many credible websites link to yours, Google treats this as a signal that your site is valuable and trustworthy. But beware - link quality is more important than quantity. One link from a credible industry website is worth more than 100 links from random directories.
offpage.li1: Link building - acquiring valuable links from industry portals, business directories, and media
offpage.li2: Google Business Profile - crucial for local SEO. A business profile on Google is the foundation of visibility in maps and local results
offpage.li3: NAP Citations - consistent business data (Name, Address, Phone) across various online sources builds credibility
offpage.li4: Customer reviews - positive Google reviews not only build trust but also affect rankings in local results

local.p1: If you run a service business - a restaurant, hair salon, law firm, car workshop, or marketing agency - local SEO is absolutely crucial for you. 46% of all Google searches have local intent, and "near me" is one of the most commonly added terms in queries.
local.p2: Local SEO is optimization that makes your business appear in search results for queries from a specific location. When someone searches for "marketing agency" in your city, you want to be in the top three results on Google Maps - the so-called "Local Pack" that attracts the most clicks.

mistakes.intro: We've seen hundreds of websites making the same SEO mistakes. Here are the most common problems that prevent achieving good positions in Google:
mistakes.li1: No keyword strategy - positioning without keyword analysis is like shooting blindly. You need to know what your customers are searching for
mistakes.li2: Slow website - WordPress sites with dozens of plugins often take 4-6 seconds to load. Google penalizes slow websites with lower rankings
mistakes.li3: No mobile version or poor responsiveness - over 60% of traffic comes from mobile devices. A website that doesn't work well on a phone loses most of its traffic
mistakes.li4: Content duplication - duplicating content across different pages confuses Google and can lead to penalties
mistakes.li5: Ignoring local SEO - service businesses without an optimized Google Business profile lose customers to the competition
mistakes.li6: Buying cheap links - links from link farms and spammy directories can lead to Google penalties instead of improved rankings
mistakes.highlight: The most important SEO rule: don't try to trick Google. Algorithms are getting smarter and effectively detect manipulation. Focus on creating valuable content and a technically sound website - that's a strategy that always works.

why.p1: We often hear the question: "Can't I do SEO myself?" Theoretically yes - but in practice, SEO is a complex, constantly changing field. Google updates its algorithm hundreds of times a year, and what worked a year ago might not deliver results today or could even be harmful.
why.p2: Professional SEO is an investment that pays off. Compare: a Google Ads campaign costs you exactly what you put in - turn off the ads and the traffic disappears. SEO builds organic visibility that lasts for months and years. Once you achieve good positions, traffic from Google is practically free.
why.highlight: Example: a service company invests 2000 PLN monthly in SEO. After 6 months, it starts appearing in the TOP 3 for key phrases. It generates 50 inquiries per month from Google - each worth an average of 500 PLN. That's 25,000 PLN in potential monthly revenue from a 2,000 PLN investment.

process.intro: Our approach to SEO is based on three pillars: technical website excellence, valuable content, and strategic authority building.
process.li1: SEO Audit - We start with a thorough analysis of your website, competition, and industry. We identify technical issues, content gaps, and growth opportunities.
process.li2: Keyword Strategy - We research what your potential customers are searching for. We select keywords with high conversion potential and realistic competitiveness.
process.li3: Technical Optimization - We fix technical errors, speed up the website, improve structure, and implement structured data. Our pure HTML websites have a technological advantage from the start.
process.li4: Content Creation - We write valuable blog articles, optimize existing content, and build your website's topical authority.
process.li5: Monitoring and Reporting - Every month you receive a report with rankings, traffic, and conversions. You track progress and see real results of our work.

conclusion.p1: SEO is not a luxury - it's a necessity for every business that wants to be visible online. In a world where 93% of online experiences start with a search engine, not being present on Google means losing customers to competitors who are already investing in positioning.
conclusion.p2: The key to effective SEO is combining technical website excellence with valuable content and strategic authority building. That's exactly what we do at Dekada72H - we create websites that not only look professional but above all are visible where your customers are searching.
conclusion.highlight: The best time to start SEO was a year ago. The second best time is now. The sooner you start, the sooner your business will reap the rewards of organic Google traffic.

cta.title: Want to Be Visible on Google?
cta.desc: Talk to us about positioning your website. We'll prepare a free SEO audit and show you how we can improve your search engine visibility.

article4.title: Business Process Automation - Your Company on Autopilot | Dekada72H Blog

meta.desc: Business process automation - how to save time, reduce costs, and scale your company without hiring new employees. A guide for businesses.

article4.heading: Business Process Automation - Your Company on Autopilot
article4.subheading: How to stop wasting time on repetitive tasks and let technology work for you 24 hours a day.

card.title: Business Process Automation - Your Company on Autopilot
card.excerpt: How to save dozens of hours per month and increase revenue without hiring new employees? A guide to automation for businesses.

article4.intro1: Every day you waste hours answering the same customer questions, manually sending confirmations, transcribing data from forms to spreadsheets, and checking calendars. These tasks are necessary, but do you really have to do them yourself? The answer is no. Each of these tasks can be handled by automation - 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, no holidays, no mistakes, no delays.
article4.intro2: In this article, we'll show you how much time and money the average company loses on tasks that should be automated. We'll present real automation examples and explain why companies that implement automation grow faster than their competition. As an agency with over 10 years of experience, we see this every day with our clients.

h2.problem: How Much Time Does Your Company Waste on Manual Work?
h2.what: What Can Be Automated in Your Company?
h2.examples: What Does It Look Like in Practice? Real Examples
h2.why: Why Companies Don't Automate - And Why It's a Mistake
h2.process: How Do We Implement Automation at Dekada72H?

problem.p1: Stop for a moment and count. How many times a day do you or your employees do something that could be automated? Answering contact form inquiries, sending appointment confirmations, manually creating quotes, copying data between systems, reminding customers about payments. These aren't tasks that require your talent and experience - it's purely mechanical work.
problem.p2: Studies show that the average employee spends 23 hours per week on tasks that could be automated. That's almost 3 full working days! Imagine what your company could achieve if those 3 days per week could be spent on business development, building customer relationships, or creating new services.
problem.highlight: If you employ 5 people and each loses 3 hours daily on repetitive tasks, that's 15 hours per day - almost 2 additional full-time positions - that you can recover through automation. Without hiring new employees.

stat.first: Customers choose the company that responds first

what.intro: Many companies think automation is something for large corporations with IT departments. Nothing could be further from the truth. Even a one-person business can automate key processes and operate like a company with 10 employees. Here are the areas we automate most often:
what.li1: Customer inquiry handling - A customer fills out a form on your website at 11 PM? They automatically receive a confirmation, personalized response, and suggested meeting times. You see it in the morning - everything is already organized. No more "I forgot to reply".
what.li2: Bookings and appointments - The customer chooses a time slot from your available calendar, receives automatic confirmation, a reminder 24 hours before the visit, and an SMS on the day of the meeting. Zero phone calls, zero manual confirmations.
what.li3: Answering frequently asked questions - An intelligent chatbot on your website answers 80% of customer questions instantly - about prices, availability, location, working hours. Your team only handles complex inquiries.
what.li4: Data collection and organization - Every form inquiry, every phone call, every email automatically goes into one system. No more searching for information in your inbox, notebook, and sticky notes on your desk.
what.li5: Reports and notifications - Every morning you receive an automatic summary: how many inquiries came in, how many appointments were booked, what tasks are waiting. Without logging into 5 different systems.
what.li6: Customer follow-ups - A customer received a quote but didn't respond? After 3 days, they automatically get a polite reminder. After a week - a second message asking if they need help. This isn't spam - it's professional customer service.

caption.team: Automation frees your team from repetitive tasks and lets them focus on what truly matters.
caption.dashboard: Automation gives you a complete picture of what's happening in your company - in real time.
caption.planning: Every automation starts with understanding your business and the processes that can be streamlined.

examples.intro: To better understand the power of automation, let's look at four typical scenarios we implement for our clients:
examples.highlight: The common denominator of these stories? None of these companies hired a new employee. None bought expensive software. None changed how they work. They simply let technology take over the tasks that had been eating their time.

h3.example1: Beauty Salon - No More Phone Calls About Bookings
h3.example2: Renovation Company - Professional Service Without a Secretary
h3.example4: Toy Store - Automated Order Processing and Parent Notifications

example1.p1: Before automation: the owner spent 2 hours daily answering phones, writing appointments in a notebook, and confirming schedules via SMS. Customers called during treatments, so some inquiries were ignored - meaning lost customers.
example1.p2: After automation: customers book appointments online 24/7, receiving automatic confirmations and reminders. The owner recovered 2 hours daily and stopped losing customers who couldn't get through by phone. Result: 35% more bookings in the first month.

example2.p1: Before automation: form inquiries sat in the inbox for hours because the owner was at a construction site. Half the customers went to competitors who responded faster.
example2.p2: After automation: every inquiry immediately generates a personalized response with an estimated timeline and a request for photos. Data goes into the system, and the owner sees a sorted list of new clients with complete information in the evening. Result: response time dropped from 6 hours to 30 seconds.

example3.p1: Before automation: potential clients filled out the contact form, but follow-up depended on the secretary's memory. Some leads "disappeared" - nobody called back, nobody sent reminders.
example3.p2: After automation: every lead immediately enters the system, receives an automatic response, is assigned to the appropriate lawyer, and tracked until case closure. If the client doesn't respond - the system sends a follow-up automatically. Result: 50% more conversions from inquiries to clients.

example4.p1: Before automation: the owner manually confirmed every order by email, checked product availability in the warehouse, and sent customers shipment status updates. During the holiday season, order processing took the entire day - there was no time for customer consulting or ordering new products.
example4.p2: After automation: the system automatically confirms orders, updates inventory levels, and sends parents notifications at every stage - from confirmation through packaging to delivery. When a popular product runs low, the system automatically generates a supplier order. During the holiday season, the owner handled 3 times more orders without additional staff. Result: 40% more sales in December with the same number of people.

why.intro: We've been hearing the same objections for years. Here are the most common excuses - and why each of them is wrong:
why.li1: "It's probably very expensive" - Automation costs a fraction of the monthly salary of an employee doing the same tasks manually. It pays for itself in the first month
why.li2: "My company is too small for automation" - Small businesses actually benefit the most, because every saved hour matters enormously when you do everything yourself
why.li3: "Customers prefer human contact" - Customers prefer a fast response. Automation doesn't replace contact - it ensures the customer gets an immediate reaction, and you can dedicate conversation time when it's truly needed
why.li4: "I don't understand technology" - That's exactly why we exist. We configure everything for you. You use a ready-made solution - as simple as checking your email
why.li5: "Quality will suffer" - On the contrary. Automation eliminates human errors, forgetfulness, and delays. Every customer receives the same professional service - whether it's Monday morning or Saturday evening

advantage.p1: Think about it from the customer's perspective. They search for a service online, fill out a contact form on two websites - yours and your competitor's. Your competitor responds the next morning. You - thanks to automation - respond in 30 seconds with a professional message, propose meeting times, and send your portfolio. Who wins that customer?
advantage.p2: Response speed is now one of the most important factors in choosing a company. Studies show that the company that responds first wins the customer 78% of the time. Automation ensures you always respond first - even at 3 AM, even on holidays, even when you're on vacation.
advantage.highlight: Automation is not a question of "if" but "when". Your competition is already doing it or just starting. Every day without automation is a day you're losing customers to companies that respond faster, operate more efficiently, and serve more professionally - not because they have more employees, but because they have better systems.

process.intro: Our process is simple and painless. You don't need to know anything about technology - that's what we're here for. Here's how it works:
process.li1: Process Analysis - We discuss your business and identify the tasks that consume the most time. We map the entire process - from customer inquiry to service completion.
process.li2: Automation Design - We prepare a plan: what we automate, in what order, and what results to expect. Everything in plain language, without technical jargon.
process.li3: Implementation and Integration - We configure everything for you. We connect automation with your website, calendar, email, and other systems you use.
process.li4: Testing and Training - We test every scenario to make sure everything works perfectly. We show you how to use the new solutions - it's really simple.
process.li5: Support and Optimization - After implementation, we don't leave you alone. We monitor automation performance, analyze results, and make improvements on an ongoing basis.

roi.p1: Let's do the math with a simple example. Suppose you run a service business and receive 10 customer inquiries daily. Manually handling each inquiry - replying to email, checking calendar, sending confirmation - takes an average of 15 minutes. That's 2.5 hours daily, 12.5 hours weekly, over 50 hours monthly.
roi.p2: If your work hour is worth 100 PLN, then 50 hours monthly on manual tasks costs your company 5,000 PLN. Automating these processes can cost a fraction of that amount - and the results are immediate and lasting. And that's without counting lost customers who never received a response.
roi.highlight: Automation is not a cost - it's the highest-return investment you can make in your business. Every dollar spent on automation comes back many times over in saved time, more customers, and more professional service.

conclusion.p1: Business process automation is not the future - it's the present. Companies implementing automation today are building an advantage that becomes impossible to catch up with over time. It's not about replacing people with machines - it's about freeing people from mechanical work so they can do what they do best: build relationships, create solutions, and grow the business.
conclusion.p2: At Dekada72H, we don't sell technology - we sell time. Your time that you recover through automation to invest where it brings the most value. And we do it so that implementation is painless, understandable, and delivers results from day one.
conclusion.highlight: Ask yourself one question: if you could recover 50 hours per month and simultaneously improve your customer service quality - would that change your business? If the answer is "yes" - let's talk.
